title: Decide Meshtastic Position policy for time-only GNSS snapshots
description: Follow-up from review of project-LICHEN-t2hn.6.3. Current Meshtastic NodeInfo omits the entire Position message unless both latitude and longitude are valid, so valid GNSS fix_time_unix, altitude, or satellites without coordinates are intentionally not sent to mobile clients. This avoids ambiguous Position payloads but should be confirmed against app compatibility expectations and documented or changed.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded for whether Meshtastic Position may be emitted with time-only, altitude-only, or satellites-only data. If allowed, codec and gateway adapter tests cover those cases; if forbidden, the policy is documented in code comments or docs and tests assert omission.
notes:
2026-07-02 no-hardware implementation evidence:
- Decision: Meshtastic NodeInfo.position is forbidden for time-only, altitude-only, satellites-only, latitude-only, or longitude-only snapshots. Position is emitted only when both latitude and longitude are valid. Optional altitude, fix_time_unix, and satellites are included only inside that location-bearing Position.
- Code/docs: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshtastic/codec.c now documents the lat+lon gate; docs/meshtastic-compat-dev.md documents that POSITION_APP writes and peer-position announce translation are deferred and that NodeDB map positions require lat/lon.
- Tests: lichen/tests/meshtastic_codec/src/main.c covers time-only, altitude-only, satellites-only, lat-only, and lon-only omission. lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter/src/main.c covers HAL time-only, altitude-only, and satellites-only omission through want_config NodeInfo sync.
- Local checks: git diff --check passed.
- Code review: Herschel final review no P0/P1/P2 findings; Ampere final re-review confirmed prior doc P2 findings fixed and no P0/P1/P2 findings remain.
- EC2 validation: instance i-0c8e0ce7b9e897677 in us-east-2a using EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58. Bootstrap-host hit known Ubuntu Noble awscli package gap; installed required build deps directly, sourced /mnt/lichen-zephyr/env.sh with Zephyr v3.7.0 and west v1.5.0. Primary EBS worktree was dirty/behind, so validation used clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-t2hn-6-3-3-validate based on origin/main plus local patch.
- Twister command: west twister -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_codec -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --outdir twister-out-t2hn-6-3-3 --inline-logs --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen
- Twister result: 2 scenarios / 4 instances selected; 3 passed, 0 failed, 0 errored, 1 skipped by static filter; 94 test cases executed; 30 skipped; 0 warnings; duration 119.06s. Passed instances: meshtastic_codec native_sim 32 cases, meshtastic_codec qemu_x86 32 cases, meshtastic_gateway_adapter native_sim 30 cases. gateway_adapter qemu_x86 skipped by platform_allow/native_sim-only.
- Size output: native_sim meshtastic_codec text=72862 data=793 bss=8966 dec=82621; native_sim meshtastic_gateway_adapter text=242975 data=7115 bss=74904 dec=324994; qemu_x86 meshtastic_codec text=106888 data=3708 bss=87968 dec=198564.
- EC2 cleanup: sync completed; /mnt/lichen-zephyr unmounted; vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 detached and available; instance i-0c8e0ce7b9e897677 terminated; temporary security group sg-0906aeb6414e3df80 deleted.
close_reason: Completed: documented and tested policy forbidding partial Meshtastic Position emission without both latitude and longitude; EC2 Twister validation passed; review findings resolved.
